sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Shadow Copies Deletion Using Operating Systems Utilities
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Shadow Copies deletion using operating systems utilities
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Michael Haag, Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community, Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades)

values.Description: Command Line Interface for Microsoft\xae Volume Shadow Copy Service

crowdsourced_yara_results.author: Felix Bilstein - yara-signator at cocacoding dot com
Felix Bilstein - yara-signator at cocacoding dot com

attributes.magic: PE32 executable (GUI) Intel 80386, for MS Windows

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis: The binary is identified as Ragnar Locker ransomware. It exhibits classic ransomware behavior: it first checks the system's locale to avoid execution in CIS countries (e.g., Russian, Ukrainian). It then deletes volume shadow copies using `vssadmin delete shadows /all /quiet` and `wmic.exe shadowcopy delete` to prevent file recovery. The malware enumerates and terminates processes and services to unlock files for encryption. It traverses all drives, encrypting files with a custom stream cipher (likely Salsa20) in a multi-threaded manner, and appends a `.ragnar_<hash>` extension. A ransom note named `RGNR_<hash>.txt` containing a `---RAGNAR SECRET---` section is created on the Public Desktop and then displayed to the user by launching `notepad.exe`.
